reply_content: We’re sorry to hear this. Medito is a non-profit, run mostly by volunteers, offering free meditation for all. Unlike paid apps like Waking Up, we rely on donations, not subscriptions, to stay ad-free and offset our recurring costs. Comparing us to a premium service isn’t fair, we only have 2 devs. But we respect your choice and wish you the best.
content: I have been a huge supporter of Medito for ~2 years, but I have grown tired and dissapointed with the developers. I have been donating for ~year and I have cancelled my donation today. I have moved to Waking Up, where I will pay more, but I can expect a professional approach without the app saying how they are about to run out of money AGAIN.

content: I am so grateful to Medito for its excellent support in falling asleep. After years of insomnia and poor sleep habits, listening to their bedtime meditations has allowed me to drift off easily without racing thoughts or worry.

I've been using this app for over 3 years now and my life has significantly improved. The meditation timer and streak functions are my most used features but far from the greatest reason to use the app. Upon going through the courses you learn meditative and spiritual ideas in a friendly, understandable and non-preachy way. It's all compatible with religions as well, hence you can find meditative insights as a Muslim or Christian without hurting your relationship to God.

content: I agree with your vision of providing free knowledge about meditation. It's a good thing you're doing. 😄 Edit: Bug fixed, it's loading now. The interface is simple and easy to use. The course content as I've seen so far is meaningful. I like the "emergency" feature. Haven't tried all the options yet, but I look forward to checking everything out! I know it's going to be wonderfully helpful for maintaining my mental health once I get a habit going.

I appreciate the app's thoughtful design and diverse meditation options, catering to Spanish speakers, heartbreak, students, teachers, sleep, pandemic-related stress, and more. The app's aesthetic format is pleasing, making it enjoyable to use, and the developer's efforts to enhance user experience are evident. Providing meditations for various life experiences is particularly commendable and heartwarming!!

content: January 2024 version has many improvements compared to December. Major UX improvements needed: (1) meditation sensations do not remember choices, e.g. choosing "10 mins" and reloading the app defaults to "3 mins". Then, (2) no visual difference between groups of sessions (30 day challnge) and specific session (day 1). Then, (3) no visual clue for progress of group of sessions (e.g. 30 day challenge), would be useful a 30/30 completed, or 10/30 - I mostly forget about the name of the challenge.

content: Use it for a week now and regret only not finding it earlier! It is completely free, doesn't have adds and has a very clear design, wich is easy to navigate. I also like the style - there is no agressive language of self-promotion, or unrealistic promises, like in many other apps. Instead it encourages you to try, to see what works for you, warns that it takes time. One thing I miss is the "add to queue" feature, like in Spotify, for example, to listen before sleep without opening eyes.
